goal: Add a trustworthy CSV bulk invite flow for admins without inventing row-outcome semantics during execution.

requirementBrief.problemStatement: Admins onboarding larger teams need predictable bulk invite behavior for duplicates, invalid rows, and seat limits.
requirementBrief.solutionSummary: Freeze deterministic CSV row outcomes before executing the bulk invite flow.

userStories.want: upload a CSV of invite emails
userStories.benefit: invite many collaborators without one-by-one entry
acceptance: Mixed valid rows produce visible accepted outcomes

userStories.want: see duplicate, invalid, and over-limit row states
userStories.benefit: understand what happened without support help
acceptance: Every skipped or rejected row has a reason

edgeOrRecoveryStories.boundary: CSV contains duplicates, invalid emails, or over-limit rows
edgeOrRecoveryStories.desiredOutcome: safe rows can proceed while bad rows are explained
acceptance: Rule matrix covers duplicate, invalid, and seat-limit cases

implementationDecisions: Freeze one row-outcome matrix before execution resumes
testingDecisions: Test row semantics through bulk-import rules and the admin upload flow
furtherNotes: Design remains blocked until duplicate and seat-limit semantics are approved

knownEvidence: Best-effort upload would hide duplicate, invalid, and seat-limit semantics
Audit mapping depends on deterministic row outcomes
decisionQuestions.recommendation: Choose Option B and freeze a rule matrix first

options.label: Front-end-only CSV upload
options.good: Fastest prototype and reuses the existing single-invite API
options.costRisk: Lets hidden backend edge cases define user-visible semantics

options.label: Rule matrix first
options.good: Makes billing, duplicate, invalid, and audit behavior reviewable
options.costRisk: Requires one more planning pass before execution can start

decisionQuestions.impact: cc-do stays blocked until row outcomes are modeled

knownEvidence: Duplicate and seat-limit outcomes are still not explicit enough for tests
Audit entries must match visible row outcomes
decisionQuestions.recommendation: Answer the row-outcome matrix before task generation

options.label: Block execution until matrix is explicit
options.good: Prevents cc-do from inventing semantics during implementation
options.costRisk: Requires user/product input before coding can continue

options.label: Let implementation choose defaults
options.good: Starts coding sooner with fewer planning minutes
options.costRisk: Creates invisible product decisions inside implementation tasks

decisionQuestions.impact: No executable task manifest should be approved until this is answered

tasks.title: [TEST] Add failing tests for duplicate and over-limit row outcomes
acceptance: dangerous row outcomes are reproduced as explicit failing tests

notes: freeze row semantics before broader execution

tasks.title: [IMPL] Implement the initial bulk invite row classification
acceptance: bulk invite rows receive deterministic initial states

notes: do not invent partial-failure semantics outside the approved design

tasks.title: [TEST] Add failing admin panel tests for mixed CSV results
checks: admin panel tests fail before UI integration
acceptance: mixed row outcomes are visible as UI failures before implementation

notes: UI must not hide ambiguous row states

tasks.title: [IMPL] Render bulk invite results in the admin panel
acceptance: admins can see accepted, skipped, and rejected rows in the panel

notes: surface row states without redefining backend truth

tasks.title: Run mixed-surface checks and gather review evidence
acceptance: fresh verification evidence exists for cc-check

notes: fresh evidence can still block if the design contract is incomplete
